message:
fix(hedge): clamp raw atomic estimate in observe() CAS loop + concurrency tests

Clamp the next estimate value to [min_delay, max_delay] inside the
observe() CAS loop, before writing to the atomic. Previously,
clamping only happened at config projection time (current_config()),
meaning concurrent readers of estimate_nanos could observe
out-of-range values. This was benign for HedgeConfig consumers but
violated the invariant that the stored state is always bounded.

Add raw_estimate_nanos() test helper and 4 new tests:
  - clamps_raw_atomic_state_at_upper_bound: huge sample clamped to max
  - clamps_raw_atomic_state_at_lower_bound_after_decay: repeated zero
    samples decay to min_delay
  - keeps_atomic_state_bounded_under_multithreaded_observe_contention:
    4 threads × 512 observe() calls with mixed samples; assert raw
    atomic never escapes bounds after each observe

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
